skill.name: anki-card-creator
skill.description: Generate Anki-importable flashcards from medical study content including lecture notes, drug summaries, and anatomy materials.
skill.summary: Converts medical study materials into structured Anki flashcards with a reproducible, assumption-tracked workflow.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged 55 potential issues with a risk score of 100/100, but all high-severity detections are false positives: 'weak cryptographic algorithm' and 'system reconnaissance' hits are on markdown documentation and audit JSON content with no actual crypto or recon code present; 39 of 40 'backtick execution' hits are markdown code fences. After semantic review, the skill is a legitimate Anki flashcard generator for medical content with no malicious code, no network access, and no credential handling. The only genuine concerns are minor: unvalidated file I/O paths in the Python CLI script, which is standard for this type of tool. Risk level set to LOW.

medium_findings.title: Unvalidated output file path
medium_findings.description: The export_anki_format method writes to a user-specified output path from the --output argument without validating that the path stays within the workspace directory. An attacker could supply an arbitrary filesystem path via the CLI.

medium_findings.title: Unvalidated input file path
medium_findings.description: The script reads from a user-supplied input file path via --input without path validation, potentially allowing access to files outside the intended workspace directory.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URL in documentation
low_findings.description: SKILL.md contains a hardcoded GitHub URL linking to the source repository. This is a documentation attribution link with no runtime network behavior.

low_findings.title: External command references in documentation
low_findings.description: SKILL.md and audit-reference.md contain Python CLI commands in markdown code blocks as usage examples. These are documentation snippets for the user to run manually, not automated execution by the skill.

dangerous_patterns.title: File write from user-controlled path
dangerous_patterns.description: The script accepts --output from CLI arguments and writes directly to that path without validation or sandboxing, allowing writes to arbitrary filesystem locations.

dangerous_patterns.title: File read from user-controlled path
dangerous_patterns.description: The script accepts --input from CLI arguments and opens that path for reading without validation, potentially enabling unauthorized file access.

dangerous_patterns.title: Security checklist cites path traversal prohibition
dangerous_patterns.description: The security checklist at SKILL.md:128 includes a checkbox item explicitly prohibiting path traversal (No unauthorized file system access). This is a defensive posture, not a vulnerability, but the static analyzer flagged the '../' text pattern in the checklist entry.

content.user_title: Create Anki Flashcards from Medical Study Content
content.value_statement: Converting medical notes into structured flashcards is time-consuming and error-prone. This skill generates Anki-importable cards from your study content with a reproducible, assumption-tracked workflow.
actual_capabilities: Convert Q&A formatted text files into Anki-importable TSV flashcards
Generate drug study cards with mechanism, indication, and side-effect fields
Generate anatomy study cards with structure, location, and function fields
Export cards in tab-separated format ready for direct import into the Anki desktop application
Provide demo flashcards when no input file is supplied, showing expected output format
Produce manual card blueprints with explicit assumptions when script execution is unavailable
limitations: Requires structured Q&A or field-based input for reliable card generation
Raw prose input needs manual curation and reformatting before conversion
Does not support image or media embedding in generated flashcards
Output quality depends entirely on input quality and completeness

use_cases.title: Medical Student Exam Review
use_cases.description: Convert lecture notes and textbook excerpts into atomic recall cards for spaced-repetition exam preparation.

use_cases.title: Pharmacology Flashcard Creation
use_cases.description: Generate drug mechanism, indication, and side-effect cards from pharmacology references and drug summaries.

use_cases.title: Anatomy Study Material
use_cases.description: Create structure-location-function cards from anatomy textbooks and dissection guides.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Q&A Card Generation
prompt_templates.prompt: Convert the following Q&A formatted study notes into Anki flashcards using the anki-card-creator workflow. Validate that each card has a clear front and back before exporting.
prompt_templates.scenario: User has prepared a Q&A text file ready for conversion

prompt_templates.title: Drug Card Creation
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an Anki drug card for [drug name]. Include the mechanism of action, clinical indications, and major side effects. Export the result as a TSV file.
prompt_templates.scenario: User wants to create pharmacology review cards

prompt_templates.title: Anatomy Card Creation
prompt_templates.prompt: Create anatomy flashcards for [structure name]. Cover its anatomical location, primary function, and clinical correlations. Output as Anki-importable TSV.

prompt_templates.title: Batch Processing with Validation
prompt_templates.prompt: Process this input file of medical study content and generate Anki flashcards. For each card, verify that the front asks a single clear question and the back provides a complete answer. Flag any cards that need expert review before import.
prompt_templates.scenario: User has a large batch of study material to process

output_examples.input: Convert this Q&A: What is the mechanism of Metformin?
output: Front: What is the mechanism of Metformin?
Back: Inhibits hepatic gluconeogenesis and increases insulin sensitivity

output_examples.input: Create a drug card for Aspirin with mechanism, indications, and side effects
output: Back: Aspirin - Irreversibly inhibits COX-1 and COX-2 enzymes. Indications: Pain, fever, inflammation, antiplatelet therapy. Side effects: GI bleeding, tinnitus, Reye syndrome in children.

output_examples.input: Create an anatomy card for the Heart covering location and function
output: Back: Heart - Location: Mediastinum, between the lungs, slightly left of midline. Function: Pumps blood through the pulmonary and systemic circulatory systems via coordinated atrial and ventricular contractions.

best_practices: Always review generated cards for medical accuracy before importing into Anki for study use
Use structured Q&A format or field-based command-line arguments for the most reliable card output
Validate the output TSV file opens correctly in a spreadsheet program before importing into Anki
anti_patterns: Do not use raw unstructured prose as input without first reformatting into clear question and answer pairs
Do not import generated cards into Anki without reviewing the content for factual accuracy
Do not rely on the built-in demo output for actual study without verifying medical correctness with primary sources

faq.question: What input format does this skill require?
faq.answer: The skill accepts Q&A formatted text files with lines starting with Q: and A:, or structured drug and anatomy fields via command-line arguments.

faq.question: Can I use this skill without running the Python script?
faq.answer: Yes, the skill can generate a manual card blueprint with explicit assumptions when direct script execution is not available or fails.

faq.question: What output format does the skill produce?
faq.answer: It produces a tab-separated values file with front and back columns that can be directly imported into the Anki desktop application via File then Import.

faq.question: Does this skill require internet access to work?
faq.answer: No, the skill runs entirely offline. It uses only Python standard library modules with no external API calls or network dependencies.

faq.question: Can I customize the flashcard format beyond the default front and back structure?
faq.answer: The output follows the standard Anki two-field TSV format. You can modify the card template within Anki after import to adjust styling and field display.

faq.question: What happens if my input is incomplete or missing required fields?
faq.answer: The skill requests only the minimum missing fields needed instead of inventing content. It will provide a clear note about what information is required to proceed.
